search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG109 transient loss consciousness recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Presyncope is not the exact transient-loss-of-consciousness population.
Treatment guidance does not support a presentation-level documentation workflow.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for people aged 16 years and over with transient loss of consciousness, not children or presyncope without loss of consciousness.
setting_applicability: Direct for initial and specialist assessment; UAE referral and driving rules require local review.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review, local emergency pathways, and local driving requirements.
recency_verification: The current NICE CG109 recommendations, last updated 2023-11-21, were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NICE CG109 remains the current official guideline for this exact scope.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped TLoC terms, event and witness history, assessed negatives, neurological finding, examination, presets, and history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped ECG, glucose, and selected blood tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed or reviewed 12-lead ECG and selected additional tests such as glucose or haemoglobin when indicated by the assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped exertional TLoC, family sudden death, urgent referral, and cardiology follow-up.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.
Supports clinician-led orthostatic assessment, repeat or previous ECG review, ambulatory ECG selection, further investigation, and specialist cardiovascular assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped advice, safety, driving, return, and recurrence fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children, presyncope without loss of consciousness, chest-pain and palpitation red-flag labels, and fixed one-week review remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, risk classification, investigation selection, referral, and driving advice remain clinician-led and locally reviewed.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
